title: osteospermum imbricatum subsp. nervatum
intro: Osteospermum imbricatum subsp. nervatum, commonly known as Cape daisy, is a flowering plant species in the daisy family, characterized by its white to pinkish-purple daisy-like flowers and narrow, linear leaves. Traditionally, the plant has been used in folk medicine for its medicinal properties, including its anti-inflammatory and antimicrobial properties, which have been attributed to the presence of sesquiterpene lactones. The health benefits of Cape daisy include its potential to reduce inflammation and alleviate symptoms associated with respiratory conditions, such as bronchitis and asthma. In herbal preparations, Osteospermum imbricatum subsp. nervatum is often used to make teas, infusions, and tinctures, which are used to harness its medicinal properties and promote overall well-being.
